nodes.name: Fetch repo file list
nodes.notes: Replace the URL with your GitHub repo path. Add a GitHub token in HTTP Header Auth credentials (header name: Authorization, value: token YOUR_TOKEN). For local files, replace this node with a Read Binary Files node pointing to your folder.

nodes.name: Download file content
nodes.notes: Downloads the raw text of each .py file. The previous node passes download_url from the GitHub API. If using local files, skip this node — the content is already available.

nodes.name: Claude — Bug scanner
nodes.notes: Sends each Python file to Claude and gets back structured JSON bug reports. Replace YOUR_ANTHROPIC_API_KEY with your actual key, or better: create an n8n credential (HTTP Header Auth) named 'Anthropic' and reference it via the authentication field.

parameters.jsonBody:
={
  "model": "claude-sonnet-4-20250514",
  "max_tokens": 4096,
  "system": "You are a senior Python engineer specialising in test-driven development. Given a Python source file, write comprehensive pytest unit tests that cover:
1. All public functions and methods
2. Normal (happy-path) cases
3. Edge cases (empty input, None, zero, very large values)
4. Known bugs flagged in the bug report (write tests that would catch those bugs once fixed)

Rules:
- Use pytest only (no unittest)
- Use descriptive test names: test_<function>_<scenario>
- Add a brief docstring to each test explaining what it verifies
- Mock external dependencies (file I/O, network, DB) using pytest-mock
- Output ONLY valid Python code. No markdown fences, no prose.",
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": "Filename: {{ $('Filter .py files only').item.json.name }}

Source code:
```python
{{ $('Download file content').item.json.data }}
```

Bug report from analysis:
{{ $('Claude — Bug scanner').item.json.content[0].text }}"
    }
  ]
}

nodes.name: Claude — Test writer
nodes.notes: Takes the source file AND the bug report from the previous node, and writes a full pytest test file. The generated tests specifically cover the bugs found.

nodes.name: Assemble output
nodes.notes: Packages the bug report JSON and the generated test file content into clean fields. Connect this to whatever output you want: Slack message, GitHub PR, write to disk, send email, etc.
